title: Kubernetes Operators and CRDs
learningObjectives: Understand the Operator pattern and when to use it
Define Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) with OpenAPI v3 validation
Build an Operator using the Operator SDK (Go or Ansible)
Implement reconciliation loops for custom resources

theory.content:
The Operator Pattern

An Operator extends Kubernetes to automate complex, stateful applications. It encodes operational knowledge (deployment, scaling, backup, upgrade) into software. Operators use Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) to define new resource types and controllers to manage them.

Examples: Prometheus Operator, CockroachDB Operator, cert-manager, Strimzi (Kafka).

Custom Resource Definition (CRD)

apiVersion: apiextensions.k8s.io/v1
kind: CustomResourceDefinition
metadata:
  name: databases.mycompany.com
spec:
  group: mycompany.com
  versions:
    - name: v1alpha1
      served: true
      storage: true
      schema:
        openAPIV3Schema:
          type: object
          properties:
            spec:
              type: object
              properties:
                size:
                  type: integer
                  minimum: 1
                  maximum: 10
                image:
                  type: string
              required: ["size", "image"]
            status:
              type: object
  scope: Namespaced
  names:
    plural: databases
    singular: database
    kind: Database
    shortNames: [db]

Custom Resource Instance

apiVersion: mycompany.com/v1alpha1
kind: Database
metadata:
  name: my-postgres
  namespace: production
spec:
  size: 3
  image: postgres:16

Operator SDK

# Initialize an Operator project
operator-sdk init \
  --domain mycompany.com \
  --repo github.com/mycompany/db-operator

# Create an API (CRD + Controller)
operator-sdk create api \
  --group mycompany \
  --version v1alpha1 \
  --kind Database \
  --resource --controller

Reconciliation Loop

func (r *DatabaseReconciler) Reconcile(
  ctx context.Context,
  req ctrl.Request) (ctrl.Result, error) {

  db := &mycompanyv1alpha1.Database{}
  if err := r.Get(ctx, req.NamespacedName, db); err != nil {
    return ctrl.Result{}, client.IgnoreNotFound(err)
  }

  // Ensure StatefulSet exists
  sts := r.buildStatefulSet(db)
  if err := r.Create(ctx, sts); err != nil {
    return ctrl.Result{}, err
  }

  // Update status
  db.Status.Ready = true
  r.Status().Update(ctx, db)
  return ctrl.Result{}, nil
}
